# timew-sync-server

NixOS service module exposing `config.services.timew-sync-server` options; wraps `pkgs.timew-sync-server` binary into two systemd units handling first-run init and the persistent sync daemon.

## Contents

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| [default.nix](./default.nix) | Declares all four `mkOption` entries, two systemd units (`timew-sync-server-init`, `timew-sync-server`), system user/group, and optional firewall rule via `lib.mkMerge` + `lib.mkIf`. |

## Configuration Surface

| Option | Type | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `enable` | `bool` | `false` |
| `dataDir` | `path` | `/var/lib/timew-sync-server` |
| `port` | `int` | `8080` |
| `openFirewall` | `bool` | `false` |

## Architecture / Data Flow

1. **`timew-sync-server-init`** — oneshot unit; gates on `ConditionPathExists = "!${cfg.dataDir}/.is_initialized"`; creates `${cfg.dataDir}/authorized_keys`, sets ownership, touches `.is_initialized`.
2. **`timew-sync-server`** — simple unit; `after = network-online.target`; `Restart = on-failure`; runs as `timew-sync-server` system user/group.

## Behavioral Contracts

**`ExecStart` pattern (verbatim):**
```
${pkgs.timew-sync-server}/bin/timew-sync-server start --port ${cfg.port} --keys-location ${cfg.dataDir}/authorized_keys --sqlite-db ${cfg.dataDir}/db.sqlite
```

**Firewall rule:** `networking.firewall.allowedTCPPorts = [ cfg.port ]` — emitted only when both `enable && openFirewall` via `lib.mkMerge` + `lib.mkIf`.

**Init gate:** `ConditionPathExists = "!${cfg.dataDir}/.is_initialized"` — ensures init unit runs exactly once.

## File Relationships

- Module consumed by [modules/default.nix](../default.nix) alongside `taskchampion-sync-server.nix` and `modules/g13d/`.
- Depends on `pkgs.timew-sync-server` supplied via [overlay.nix](../../overlay.nix) or nixpkgs; package source likely under `pkgs/` (no `pkgs/timew-sync-server/` present — must come from overlay).
